summary: Educational assistance for spouses and dependents of disabled veterans in Hawaii.
eligibility: Spouses and dependents of veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

benefit_name: Hawaii Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Hawaii offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Hawaii veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Hawaii is typically required. Contact the Hawaii veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Hawaii Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Hawaii provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Hawaii. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Hawaii veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

summary: Property tax exemption for disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities in Hawaii.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

eligibility: Hawaii resident veterans with honorable discharge.
